title: Alyssum surculosum
taxonomy: Alyssum surculosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Alyssum, which consists of annual and biennial herbs. The family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family, comprises approximately 338 genera and 3,200 species. Alyssum surculosum is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, sandy areas. The classification of Alyssum surculosum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Brassicaceae family.

intro: Alyssum surculosum, also known as Alyssum, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white, and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Alyssum surculosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Alyssum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, coughs, and colds.
